claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP start and end months (Table 3.1a) derived as min task start and max task end per WP

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Dissemination levels: D5-02, D7-01, D7-02 set to SEN pending data access agreement finalisation; all other deliverables PU
claim_audit_results.source_ref: Not present in docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json (deliverable entries contain no dissemination_level field) or docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json. Assumed SEN for operational-domain datasets based on data sensitivity considerations.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Dissemination level classifications for D5-02, D7-01, D7-02 are not explicitly specified in any Tier 1–4 source document reviewed. The wp_structure.json deliverable objects do not include a dissemination_level field. Assignment to SEN is a project-operational assumption not directly traceable to a named source artifact; subject to revision upon review of the consortium agreement and data access agreements.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: The MAESTRO consortium comprises eight partners from seven European countries
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json (partners array country field per partner: ATU-DE, CERIA-FR, BIIS-NL, ISIA-IT, NIHS-SE, ELI-CH, BAL-FI, FIIT-ES)
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: The source document (partners.json) lists 8 distinct country codes (DE, FR, NL, IT, SE, CH, FI, ES) for the 8 partners. The section claims 'seven European countries' but the named source directly evidences 8 different countries. The numeric claim 'seven' is contradicted by the source; the correct count traceable to partners.json is 8 European countries.

claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json (cycle_detected: false; cycle_flags: []; critical_path_analysis.cycle_check_method: 'Kahn's topological sort — all 36 nodes processed to completion; zero nodes remained with in-degree > 0 after queue exhaustion, confirming DAG structure'; dependency_map.nodes array contains exactly 36 elements: WP1–WP9 [9] plus T1-01 through T9-03 [27])

claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json (dependency_map.edges: WP2→WP5, WP3→WP5, WP4→WP5, WP2→WP6, WP3→WP6, WP4→WP6, WP2→WP7, WP3→WP7, WP4→WP7 — all edge_type: 'data_input'; exactly 9 edges from pillar WPs to demonstrator WPs)

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Nine paths of equal length (3 edges) exist through all combinations of pillar and demonstrator WPs
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json (critical_path_analysis.tied_paths: array of 9 paths each of 3-edge length [WP2/WP3/WP4]→[WP5/WP6/WP7]→WP8→WP9; critical_path_analysis.path_length_edges: 3)

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: BAL contributes as production-grade LLM engineering support across WP2, WP3, WP4, WP5, WP6, and WP7
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json (partner_role_matrix BAL wps_as_contributor: ['WP1','WP2','WP3','WP5','WP6','WP7','WP8'] — WP4 absent; WP4 contributing_partners: ['ATU','BIIS'] — BAL absent)
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: The section (B.3.2) claims BAL contributes across WP2, WP3, WP4, WP5, WP6, and WP7. However, wp_structure.json (authoritative Phase 3 output, schema_id: orch.phase3.wp_structure.v1) does not list BAL as a contributing partner for WP4: WP4.contributing_partners is ['ATU','BIIS'] and BAL's partner_role_matrix.wps_as_contributor excludes WP4. This is an internal inconsistency: B.3.1 correctly states WP4 contributing partners as ATU and BIIS (consistent with wp_structure.json), but B.3.2 adds BAL to WP4, which is contradicted by the named source. The claim of BAL contributing to WP4 is unresolved.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: NIHS leads the Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json (governance_matrix Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee: composition ['ATU','NIHS'], no single named lead; management_roles MGR-011 'Ethics Compliance Coordinator' assigned_to 'ATU'; MGR-012 'Healthcare Ethics Oversight Officer' assigned_to 'NIHS')
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: The section (B.3.2) states 'NIHS leads WP5 (healthcare demonstrator) and the Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee'. WP5 leadership by NIHS is confirmed by wp_structure.json. However, the claim that NIHS 'leads' the Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee is not directly supported by the source: implementation_architecture.json shows the committee is co-composed of ATU and NIHS with no designated single lead, and ATU holds the Ethics Compliance Coordinator role (MGR-011) while NIHS holds only the Healthcare Ethics Oversight Officer role (MGR-012). The characterisation of NIHS as leading the committee overstates NIHS's role relative to ATU and is not confirmed by the named source artifact.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Three-phase project structure: Foundation (M1–M12), Implementation (M13–M36), Validation and Dissemination (M37–M48)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: Phase structure naming and month boundaries not explicitly stated in any cited Tier 3 or Tier 4 source document (wp_structure.json, gantt.json, implementation_architecture.json, budget_gate_assessment.json, selected_call.json, partners.json, roles.json). Phase boundary timing is broadly consistent with gantt.json milestone structure (MS5 at M12, MS6 at M24, MS9 at M42) but the named three-phase framing is not documented.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: The named three-phase project structure (Foundation, Implementation, Validation and Dissemination) with specific month boundaries is a project-narrative characterisation not explicitly stated in any Tier 1–4 source artifact reviewed. While the inferred phase boundaries are broadly consistent with gantt.json milestone timing, the specific phase names and boundary months (M1–M12, M13–M36, M37–M48) are not attributable to a named source. This is a structural narrative assumption.
